perturbation.original_text: “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this shall not be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom the Bank rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services during Employee’s tenure.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Customers" includes a one-year look-back period and specific criteria for inclusion (material contact, management responsibility, access to Confidential Information). The new definition is narrower in look-back period and removes criteria that the employee obtained confidential information. This contradiction becomes problematic in Section 5 because it's unclear which definition of "Customer" applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Customers' narrows the scope by removing the one-year look-back period and the criteria regarding confidential information. This directly conflicts with Section 5, which relies on the original, broader definition of 'Customers' because Section 5 discusses non-solicitation of 'Customers and Potential Customers' and directly refers to the advantageous familiarity and personal contacts that the employee has with Customers. Because of the narrower definition in the Changed Text, if the employee has familiarity with former customers from the previous year before the employee's termination, those individuals may no longer qualify as a 'Customer' under the updated definition, leading to a contradiction in obligations and uncertainty around enforcement. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee acknowledges that First Financial will suffer irreparable damage and might not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach by Employee. First Financial or its assigns and successors, may be entitled to a restraining order with showing damage or bond.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "Banking Organization" with "First Financial" limits the scope of protection only to First Financial, excluding the Bank, HopFed, and Heritage. The agreement is also less assured in its enforcement language now using 'might not' and 'may be'. By limiting the scope of the 'Banking Organization', and making it only 'First Financial', this creates confusion on whether the other 'Banking Organizations', such as the 'Bank' can claim protection under Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee acknowledges that First Financial will suffer irreparable damage and might not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach by Employee. First Financial or its assigns and successors, may be entitled to a restraining order with showing damage or bond.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Banking Organization' with 'First Financial', limiting the scope of protection to only First Financial. The original text covers a broader set of organizations. The changed text also uses less assertive language ('might not', 'may be'). The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, therefore, they are in contradiction with the original text which offers a broader scope of protection.

perturbation.original_text: In any action that is brought to enforce or interpret this Agreement, the prevailing party or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to recover their reasonable attorneys’ and paralegal fees and expenses incurred in connection therewith. If a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, institute any action or proceeding to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, Employee hereby waives all claims or defenses that a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, have an adequate remedy at law, and Employee shall not urge in any such action or proceeding the claim or defense that such a remedy at law exists.
perturbation.changed_text: In any action that is brought to interpret this Agreement, the prevailing party shall recover attorneys’ fees. If the Bank institute any action, Employee does not waive defenses that the Bank have an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the attorney's fees clause to interpretation actions only and removing the waiver of adequate remedy at law defenses only applies to the 'Bank'. By specifically limiting the reach to just 'Bank', it creates ambiguity and unfair advantage to the other parties involved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Enforcement/Attorneys’ Fees . In any action that is brought to interpret this Agreement, the prevailing party shall recover attorneys’ fees. If the Bank institute any action, Employee does not waive defenses that the Bank have an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the attorney's fees clause to interpretation actions only and removes the waiver of adequate remedy at law defenses, and this only applies to the 'Bank'. The original text and contradicted text applies to "prevailing party or its assigns and successors," and "Banking Organization or its assigns and successors". This creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity and appears to unfairly advantage other parties by only addressing the bank's responsibilities. The explanation provided explicitly states this, so the change creates a contradiction in the document.

perturbation.original_text: Employee represents and warrants that he/she is not a party to an agreement with his or her prior employer(s) (other than Heritage or HopFed) or any other Person that prohibits Solicitation of business or competing with that employer or other Person. Employee affirms and represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for a Financial Institution does not, and will not, breach any agreement or covenant not to Solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence proprietary or confidential information acquired in confidence or in trust prior to Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution. Employee further represents that he/she has not retained any documents or information relating to Employee’s prior employers (other than Heritage or HopFed) that may be considered confidential or proprietary information and that Employee has not disclosed or used, and will not disclose or use, any information relating to his or her prior employer(s) in connection with Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for the Bank will not breach any agreement or covenant not to solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence information acquired prior to Employee’s employment with the Bank.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause is comprehensive, covering agreements with *any* prior employer (excluding Heritage and HopFed) and prohibiting solicitation, competition, and disclosure of confidential information. By changing it to agreements *only* related to information before joining the Bank is a significant change. This creates a contradiction: the employee can exploit confidential information from employers *other* than prior to joining the Bank without breaching this representation, which directly undermines the purpose of the Non-Disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22. Representations. Employee represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for the Bank will not breach any agreement or covenant not to solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence information acquired prior to Employee’s employment with the Bank.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly covers agreements with prior employers (excluding Heritage and HopFed), addressing solicitation, competition, and confidential information disclosure. The changed text narrows this to agreements specifically related to information acquired *before* the Employee's employment with the Bank. This change allows the employee to potentially exploit confidential information gained from employers *other than* those prior to the Bank without breaching the representation. This directly contradicts the purpose and scope of the non-disclosure obligations implied elsewhere, which are meant to protect the Bank from the misuse of competitive information, creating a strong contradiction with the contradicted section (22. Representations) which uses the same language as the Changed Text.
